'''Liam Byrne''' (born 18 August 1999) is an [[List of Ireland national rugby league team results|Ireland]] international [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|pr}} forward for the [[Wigan Warriors]] in the [[Super League]].

He has spent time on loan from [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] at [[Workington Town]] in [[2017 League 1|the Betfred League 1]], as well as the [[Leigh Centurions]] and the [[Swinton Lions]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]].

==Background==
Byrne was born in [[Salford]], [[Greater Manchester]], England. He is of Irish heritage.

==Career==
===Wigan Warriors===
In 2019, he made his [[Super League]] debut for [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] against [[Hull F.C.]]<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/match/EVP2921681|title=Super League: Wigan Warriors v Hull FC|publisher=BBC}}</ref><ref name="rugbyleagueproject">{{cite web|url=http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/players/liam-byrne/summary.html|title=Liam Byrne - Career Stats & Summary|website=Rugby League Project|accessdate=2019-03-02}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.leighobserver.co.uk/sport/wigan-warriors-loan-crisis-hit-leigh-four-players-for-final-1-9379540|title=Wigan Warriors loan crisis-hit Leigh FOUR players for final|website=www.leighobserver.co.uk|language=en|access-date=2019-03-06}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.pitchero.com/clubs/cadisheadrhinosarlfc/news/liam-byrne-signs-for-wigan-warriors-1791189.html|title=Liam Byrne signs for Wigan Warriors - News - Cadishead Rhinos|website=www.pitchero.com|language=en|access-date=2019-03-06}}</ref>

On 28 May 2022, Byrne played for Wigan in their [[2022 Challenge Cup Final]] victory over [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/61594770.amp/|title=Challenge Cup final: Huddersfield Giants 14-16 Wigan Warriors|website=www.bbc.co.uk|date=26 May 2022 }}</ref>
On 24 February 2024, Byrne played in Wigan's [[2024 World Club Challenge]] final victory over [[Penrith Panthers|Penrith]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/68337685|title=World Club Challenge: Wigan Warriors 16-12 Penrith Panthers - Cherry and Whites crowned world champions for fifth time|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>
In the 2024 Good Friday game against arch-rivals [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]], Byrne was shown a straight red card for a dangerous high tackle in Wigan's 12-4 loss. He was later suspended for four games and fined £750.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/68708760|title=Liam Byrne: Wigan Warriors prop gets four-game ban for making head contact|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>
On 12 October 2024, Byrne played in Wigan's 9-2 2024 Super League Grand Final victory over Hull Kingston Rovers.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/live/2024/oct/12/wigan-v-hull-kr-super-league-grand-final-2024-live-score-updates?page=with:block-670aaacb8f087ed1d5a8a350/|title=Wigan v Hull KR: Super League Grand Final 2024 – as it happened|website=www.theguardian.com}}</ref>
On 9 October 2025, Byrne played in Wigan's 24-6 2025 Super League Grand Final loss against Hull Kingston Rovers.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cx2jw497jpzo/|title=Hull KR beat Wigan in Grand Final to complete treble|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>

===Warrington Wolves===
On 10 September 2025 it was reported that he had signed for [[Warrington Wolves]] on a two-year deal<ref>{{cite web|website=BBC Sport|date=10 Sep 2025|accessdate=10 Oct 2025|title=Wigan prop Byrne to join Warrington from 2026|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/c75qzdqp5p9o}}</ref>
